Standard Description: Reddish watery discharge from meat, called the 'purge,' is mostly water stained by red muscle cells (myoglobin), not blood. Anything that breaks/disrupts the integrity of the muscle cells would cause more purge.
internet_ref_description: Purge indicates moisture loss due to cell damage, often from freezing/thawing cycles, which affects meat quality.

manual_ref: Page 2: 'Temperature abuse: This covers a variety of possible issues... for frozen products these might include premature ripening, freezer burn, frosting and textural changes.'
Page 3: 'Frosting appears when a product is thawed and then partially refrozen.'
manual_ref_description: Heavy frosting confirms significant temperature abuse, leading to repeated thawing and refreezing, severely affecting product quality and potentially causing freezer burn.
internet_ref_links: Source Name: Freezing and Food Safety - USDA
Standard Description: The quality of frozen foods can suffer with lengthy freezer storage or fluctuating temperatures.
internet_ref_description: Pronounced frost and ice crystal formation are indicators of quality deterioration due to freeze-thaw cycles or prolonged improper storage.

Standard Description: Have a strictly enforced seal program. Identify and require seal specifications (e.g., tamper-proof, numbered seals, etc.). Inspect shipping containers closely for any evidence of tampering.

Standard Description: Rejected Shipment. Official Contracting Officer determination, upon consideration of an SDA or ITO refused shipment request with regard to out-of-condition USDA Foods. A shipment that has been rejected must leave the consignee's property when/if requested by the consignee.

manual_ref: Page 2: 'If the temperature of chilled meat falls below -1.4 °C during storage it can cause muscle cell rupture due to the slow formation of large ice crystals. This in turn leads to blood serum separation making the meat less attractive to the customer and more susceptible to microbiological deterioration.'
manual_ref_description: The reasons for rejection ('Thawed / Leaky / Bad smell') are consistent with severe temperature abuse and spoilage outlined in the manual's discussion of frozen meat issues.
